symptom: drone re-emits file_read on a path already in session context; existing nudge ('You already have X from earlier file_read') is advisory — the actual file_read still runs and re-dumps the full content
root_cause: the dedup nudge was added without a dispatch-level skip; drone sees the nudge BUT also gets the full file content again, which trains it to ignore the nudge
fix_approach: short-circuit the dispatch when path is in the _files_already_read cache: synthesize a cached-skip tool_result (is_error=False) and return before the tool executes. Cache invalidation via file_write/file_edit stays as-is.
principle: v5 — convention beats instruction. The old nudge asked; the new skip enforces.

desc: agent.py short-circuits the file_read dispatch when path is cached
agent.py coalesces path and file_path for the dedup lookup
cache invalidation path still fires on file_write / file_edit
the short-circuit records the skipped read in _tool_history so loop_guard still sees the attempt
the old advisory nudge copy is removed — no add_system_note firing the redundant text
